title: Orange Zest Bread Pudding
description: This nutty, dense German dish features orange juice, breadcrumbs and a touch of sweetness in a comforting bread pudding base.

ingredients.name: 1 loaf day-old bread
ingredients.notes: preferably day-old to allow for more absorption of the filling

ingredients.name: 1/2 cup breadcrumbs
ingredients.notes: all-purpose or store-bought breadcrumbs are fine, make sure they're dry

ingredients.name: 3 tablespoons unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: at room temperature for easier spreading and melting

ingredients.name: Juice of 1 orange
ingredients.notes: preferably fresh, at least 1/2 cup minimum required for flavoring the pudding base

ingredients.name: 3 tablespoons sugar
ingredients.notes: can be reduced to 2 tbsp if you prefer less sweetness

ingredients.name: Dash of salt
ingredients.notes: optional, can taste it out before adding more

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup heavy cream
ingredients.notes: may be substituted with milk for non-dairy option

ingredients.name: 1 teaspoon grated orange zest
ingredients.notes: freshly grated, if unavailable use 1/2 tsp dried orange zest

instructions: Gently toast the day-old bread slices until golden and slightly crisp. Let them cool.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the breadcrumbs, softened butter, orange juice, sugar, salt, heavy cream, and grated orange zest. Mix well with a spoon or spatula.
Fold in the cooled toasted bread pieces gently to distribute evenly throughout the pudding mixture. Ensure not to overmix as you don't want the bread to become too tough or dense.

Transfer the mixture into an oven-safe dish. Cover the top with aluminum foil if needed for easier removal of the dish from the oven, but leave it slightly open for some steam to escape during baking.

Bake in preheated oven for 40 minutes. After 40 minutes, remove the foil cover and continue baking until golden brown on top and a toothpick inserted near the center comes out clean, about another 15-20 minutes depending on your oven's performance.
Let it cool slightly before serving.
